title: Characterization of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Characterization of Evangelion             Quote Post by GAP » Wed Jul 06, 2011 2:51 pm What is it about the Evangelion characters that they made them so endearing? I can only think of how Evangelion puts a lot of detail and/or perosnality that transcend the characters themselves and translate to the viewer. I don't know if the same can be said of most anime but is this true of Evangelion?

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Wed Jul 06, 2011 3:03 pm Because they're shallow, yet multi-dimensional...so basically like real people. They're not just one-dimensional. They react to their problems in a very real way, and while not always likeable, it makes them enjoyable characters. A traumatized fourteen year-old boy acting like a traumatized fourteen year-old boy is more interesting than him acting like a manly action hero. That, along with the fact that their problems are also realistic, makes them easier to empathize or sympathize with, which gives the viewer a deeper emotional attachment to the drama. Or it could just be that Asuka is hot and I have a thing for emotionally broken underage girls...but that wouldn't explain why I like Shinji...actually on second though, it would.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Jul 06, 2011 3:31 pm What is sure is that the characters are one of the greatest things about eva. They reflect problems that exist in real life, and yet, they have artistic value - just the right mix between an "interesting concept" and a "realistic person". They represent X weird theorethic concepts and still go thrugh typical teenager- or about-thirty problems, the sort of emotions you can understand/emphasize with, even find yourself in. They force you to witness their inner thoughts and yet, they never reveal al their secrets to you. Also, they all have both fatal flaws and very admirable qualities. It's that mix of attraction and repulsion that creates fascination. Also, as the Q trailers discribes them, they are "people wanting to live", their struggles are intense and their truths beautiful.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Wed Jul 06, 2011 4:47 pm What makes them great is how human they are. It adds a layer of realism not found in most other anime.

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Wed Jul 06, 2011 4:53 pm I never really paid attention to the realism of the characters. I think they're great cause they're angsty, but it's justified. Shinji has the spine of a sponge, Asuka is a self-concious bitch, Misato is a drunk resposiblity ducking whore, and Gendo is a broken shell of a man with a god-complex. These characters would be uninteresting without the justification that EVA gives us. That's what makes it interesting! They're broken, but they have a reason to be broken. Which is something I'm attempting to do with my stories.

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Wed Jul 06, 2011 8:16 pm Simple; every action they do is believable; they are completely dictated by their own free will rather than the mind of a director. Their personalities manged to achieve spontaneity in animation, and not once does it feel arbitrary or false, something that's hard to do in animation. There's more to it, but that's my opinion in a nutshell.

[]:             Quote Post by GAP » Wed Jul 06, 2011 10:56 pm It jsut amazes me how much thoguht was put into the characters. I mean you can take the characters personality at face value but there is mroe to htem that meets the eye. Same is true for real life people. Eva uses basic character archetypes used in most mecha and asks, "Just what happened in their lives that made them this?" Eva seems to asks "why" and "why not" as the same time. THe characters just hit me on a personal level, there isn't a lot of shows in any medium that can do that.

[]:             Quote Post by juaxoo » Thu Jul 07, 2011 4:09 am yeah, take the fact of how horny Misato is, damn shes even seeking sexual release from Shinji. poor Shinji

[]:             Quote Post by Viking » Thu Jul 07, 2011 4:54 am juaxoo wrote: [..] poor Shinji

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Thu Jul 07, 2011 7:15 am ^actually I thought that juaxoo was being ironic, I suppose that most of us wondered what the hell Shinji was thinking...

[]:             Quote Post by GAP » Thu Jul 07, 2011 9:05 pm ^^ I need to see that Kill Bill scene....

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Fri Jul 08, 2011 5:11 pm The characters of Evangelion are so normal they're abnormal. That, is what makes them special.

I can only think of how Evangelion puts a lot of detail and/or perosnality that transcend the characters themselves and translate to the viewer. I don't know if the same can be said of most anime but is this true of Evangelion?
